internal_issues.description: WIN-028 code_analysis.monitoring was 'hardcoded' — incorrect since WIN-028 has no monitor script at all. c2 and c3 reviews both flagged this.

WIN-028 detail_verdict_text: generic 'monitor script hardcodes target number' boilerplate was factually wrong (no script). NOAA link was generic homepage. Fixed with WIN-028-specific temporal post-hoc argument and specific Bermuda Triangle NOAA page link.
WIN-028 detail_evidence missing 152°/208° longitude-separation arithmetic and agonic-line position data. Fixed by adding both.

WIN-050 diurnal/semidiurnal conflation: 'two-bulge' language in tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, and detail_verdict_text. Two-bulge describes M2 (semidiurnal), not O1 (diurnal). Fixed in all three fields.
WIN-050 detail_evidence: '0.6% at the equator' inconsistent with WIN-049's '1.48%' for identical geometry. Fixed to 1.48%.
WIN-050 finding: 'concentrates force at one point' overstated. Fixed to 'in a localized cone beneath the sub-lunar track'.

Cross-WIN boilerplate: 16 WINs with monitoring=none contain script-behavior sentence that doesn't apply to them. WIN-028 fixed; 16 others remain (001, 013, 022, 033, 040, 041, 042, 043, 048, 052, 054, 059, 060, 063, 066, 067). Too large for single-run self-apply — open issue for systematic batch fix.
